testcase.name: build > runs "npm run build" when detectPackageManager resolves "npm"
build > runs "yarn build" when detectPackageManager resolves "yarn"
build > calls detectPackageManager with the current working directory
build > propagates an error thrown by config.runCommand
dev > server process > spawns tsx from the project node_modules/.bin with --watch src/server.ts
dev > server process > prepends --inspect=0.0.0.0:9229 to tsx args when --inspect is passed
dev > server process > passes db env vars into the tsx process environment
dev > server process > includes project node_modules/.bin in PATH for the server process
dev > database lifecycle > calls detectDatabases before starting the server
dev > database lifecycle > passes the detected config to startDatabases
dev > database lifecycle > calls server.stop() on each started database after the server exits
dev > database lifecycle > does not throw when no databases are configured
dev > port detection > defaults to port 3000 and passes it to findAvailablePort
dev > port detection > passes the port through to the server env when free
dev > port detection > uses --port as the preferred base port
dev > port detection > falls back to the port returned by findAvailablePort when the preferred one is occupied
dev > port detection > warns when falling back to a different port
dev > port detection > does not warn when the preferred port is free
dev > port detection > throws when --port is explicitly set and that port is already in use
dev > port detection > does not spawn the server when the explicit --port is already in use
dev > port detection > does not warn when --port is explicitly set and already in use (it throws instead)
dev > port detection > does not throw when --port is explicitly set and already free
dev > React / Vite integration > does not spawn vite when detectReact returns false
dev > React / Vite integration > spawns vite build --watch concurrently when detectReact returns true
dev > React / Vite integration > vite process receives the same db env vars as the server
dev > --docker flag > skips detectDatabases when --docker is set
dev > --docker flag > skips startDatabases when --docker is set
dev > --docker flag > still spawns the tsx server when --docker is set
dev > --docker flag > passes no db env vars to the tsx process when --docker is set
dev > --docker flag > does not stop any database servers on exit when --docker is set
dev > --docker flag > still spawns vite in watch mode when --docker is set and React is configured
dev > environment variable passthrough > passes shell env vars through to the tsx process
dev > environment variable passthrough > passes shell env vars through to the tsx process in --docker mode
dev > environment variable passthrough > db env vars take precedence over shell env vars of the same name
dev > environment variable passthrough > vite process receives the same shell env vars as the tsx server
dev > error handling > throws when startDatabases rejects with an Error
dev > error handling > falls back to String(e) when startDatabases rejects with a non-Error value
dev > error handling > does not spawn the server when startDatabases rejects
dev > platform-specific behavior > does not append .cmd to the tsx/vite binary names on non-Windows platforms
dev > platform-specific behavior > falls back to an empty string when process.env.PATH is unset
dev > database log/warn forwarding > forwards log and warn messages from startDatabases through to the command
start > --no-build flag > skips the build step entirely and goes straight to starting the server
start > --no-build flag > does not call detectReact when --no-build is set
start > build step (without --no-build) > runs npm run build when no yarn.lock or packageManager field exists
start > build step (without --no-build) > runs yarn build when yarn.lock is present
start > build step (without --no-build) > runs yarn build when packageManager field starts with "yarn"
start > server process > spawns node with a server.js path
start > server process > checks for server files relative to the project cwd, not the node binary path
start > server process > falls back to dist/server.js when no specific layout is detected
start > server process > uses dist/server/server.js when existsSync returns true for that path (first check)
start > server process > uses dist/src/server.js when the first check fails but the second succeeds
start > server process > passes db env vars into the server process environment
start > database lifecycle > calls detectDatabases before starting the server
start > database lifecycle > passes the detected config to startDatabases
start > database lifecycle > calls server.stop() on each started database after server exits
start > --docker flag > skips detectDatabases when --docker is set
start > --docker flag > skips startDatabases when --docker is set
start > --docker flag > still spawns the server process when --docker is set
start > --docker flag > passes no db env vars to the server process when --docker is set
start > --docker flag > still runs the build step when --docker is set without --no-build
start > --docker flag > does not stop any database servers on exit when --docker is set
start > environment variable passthrough > passes shell env vars through to the server process
start > environment variable passthrough > passes shell env vars through to the server process in --docker mode
start > environment variable passthrough > db env vars take precedence over shell env vars of the same name
start > environment variable passthrough > build commands inherit the shell environment implicitly (no explicit env stripping)
start > port detection > defaults to port 3000 and passes it to findAvailablePort
start > port detection > passes the port through to the server env when free
start > port detection > uses --port as the preferred base port
start > port detection > falls back to the port returned by findAvailablePort when the preferred one is occupied
start > port detection > warns when falling back to a different port
start > port detection > does not warn when the preferred port is free
start > port detection > throws when --port is explicitly set and that port is already in use
start > port detection > does not spawn the server when the explicit --port is already in use
start > port detection > does not warn when --port is explicitly set and already in use (it throws instead)
start > port detection > does not throw when --port is explicitly set and already free
start > React / Vite integration > does not run vite build when detectReact returns false
start > React / Vite integration > runs vite build between the main build and server startup when detectReact is true
start > React / Vite integration > skips vite build when --no-build is set even if detectReact would return true
start > error handling > throws when the build command exits with a non-zero code
start > error handling > does not start databases when the build command fails
start > error handling > throws when the vite build command exits with a non-zero code
start > error handling > throws when startDatabases rejects
start > error handling > does not spawn the server when startDatabases rejects
start > error handling > falls back to String(e) when the build process errors with a non-Error value
start > error handling > falls back to String(e) when the vite build process errors with a non-Error value
start > error handling > falls back to String(e) when startDatabases rejects with a non-Error value
start > platform-specific behavior > does not append .cmd to the vite binary name on non-Windows platforms
start > --bun flag > spawns the resolved Bun executable instead of the node executable when --bun is set
start > --bun flag > spawns a downloaded Bun binary path when resolveBunExecutable returns one
start > --bun flag > does not call resolveBunExecutable when --bun is not set
start > --bun flag > throws when resolveBunExecutable rejects (e.g. download failure)
start > --bun flag > falls back to String(e) when resolveBunExecutable rejects with a non-Error value
start > --bun flag > does not spawn the server when resolveBunExecutable rejects
start > --bun flag > forwards resolveBunExecutable log/warn messages through to the command
start > database log/warn forwarding > forwards log and warn messages from startDatabases through to the command
bunAssetName > maps darwin/x64 to bun-darwin-x64
bunAssetName > maps darwin/arm64 to bun-darwin-aarch64
bunAssetName > maps linux/x64 to bun-linux-x64
bunAssetName > maps linux/arm64 to bun-linux-aarch64
bunAssetName > maps win32/x64 to bun-windows-x64
bunAssetName > throws for win32/arm64 (unsupported)
bunAssetName > throws for an unsupported platform
getBunVersion > returns the parsed version when the command succeeds
getBunVersion > coerces extraneous output into a valid version
getBunVersion > returns undefined when the command fails (e.g. not installed)
resolveBunExecutable > returns "bun" when the system version already satisfies the minimum
resolveBunExecutable > returns "bun" when the system version exceeds the minimum
resolveBunExecutable > warns and looks elsewhere when the system version is below the minimum
resolveBunExecutable > logs when bun is not installed at all
resolveBunExecutable > reuses the highest cached version satisfying the minimum, without downloading
resolveBunExecutable > ignores cached versions below the minimum
resolveBunExecutable > falls through to the next-highest cached version when the highest one is missing its binary
resolveBunExecutable > downloads and extracts the latest release when nothing is cached
resolveBunExecutable > does not chmod on win32
resolveBunExecutable > throws when the latest release is still below the minimum version
resolveBunExecutable > throws when the "latest" redirect cannot be resolved
resolveBunExecutable > throws a descriptive error when the download request fails
resolveBunExecutable > throws a descriptive error when the archive does not contain the expected binary
startDatabases error handling > wraps a MongoDB startup failure (Error) with a descriptive message
startDatabases error handling > wraps a MongoDB startup failure (non-Error) with a descriptive message
startDatabases error handling > wraps a Redis startup failure (Error) with a descriptive message
startDatabases error handling > wraps a Redis startup failure (non-Error) with a descriptive message
startDatabases error handling > wraps a Postgres startup failure (Error) with a descriptive message
startDatabases error handling > wraps a Postgres startup failure (non-Error) with a descriptive message
startDatabases error handling > leaves the ACL datastore as mongodb when both mongodb and postgresql are configured
detectDatabases > detects mongodb from type: "mongodb" in datastores
detectDatabases > detects redis from type: "redis" in datastores
detectDatabases > detects postgresql from type: "postgresql" in datastores
detectDatabases > detects multiple database types from a single config
detectDatabases > handles single-quoted type values
detectDatabases > returns all false when config has no datastore type strings
detectDatabases > returns all false when src/config.ts does not exist
startDatabases > starts mongodb-memory-server and sets DATASTORES env vars
startDatabases > sets no env vars and spawns no processes when no databases configured
startDatabases > starts redis-memory-server and sets DATASTORES env vars
startDatabases > starts postgres-memory-server and sets DATASTORES env vars
tsBlockInsert > inserts a snippet before the closing brace of the named block
tsBlockInsert > is a no-op when idempotencyKey already appears as a depth-1 property
tsBlockInsert > inserts when idempotencyKey does not match any existing property
tsBlockInsert > throws a descriptive error when the named block does not exist
tsBlockInsert > throws a descriptive error when the block has no matching closing brace
tsBlockInsert > throws a descriptive error when the root config block has no matching closing brace
tsBlockInsert > handles an empty block
tsBlockInsert > handles escaped quotes inside string literals within the scanned block
tsBlockInsert > does not match property names inside string literals
tsBlockInsert > skips property names inside // comments when checking idempotency
tsBlockInsert > root config object (empty blockName) > inserts the snippet as a top-level sibling, not inside the first named block
tsBlockInsert > root config object (empty blockName) > inserts immediately before the closing of the .defaults({ ... }) call
tsBlockInsert > root config object (empty blockName) > is a no-op when idempotencyKey already appears as a top-level property
tsBlockInsert > root config object (empty blockName) > does not match a property of the same name nested inside a named block
tsBlockInsert > root config object (empty blockName) > throws a descriptive error when no .defaults({ ... }) call exists
tsPropertySet > replaces the value of the named property inside the block
tsPropertySet > is a no-op when the value is already the desired value
tsPropertySet > preserves all other content outside the changed property
tsPropertySet > throws when the named block does not exist
tsPropertySet > returns source unchanged when the property does not exist in the block
tsPropertySet > does not affect same-named properties inside sibling blocks
tsPropertySet > handles an object-valued property without corrupting it
tsPropertySet > skips // comments when scanning for property names
tsPropertySet > skips over a string-valued property (with an escaped quote) that appears before the target property
tsPropertySet > correctly finds the end of a string-valued target property containing an escaped quote
tsPropertySet > handles an unterminated string value at the end of the source gracefully
tsPropertySet > skips a nested object value on a property preceding the target
jsonMerge > merges dependencies into an existing package.json object
jsonMerge > patch value wins on scalar conflicts
jsonMerge > deep-merges nested objects independently
jsonMerge > replaces arrays rather than concatenating
jsonMerge > is idempotent: applying the same patch twice yields the same result
jsonMerge > does not mutate the target argument
jsonMerge > handles an empty patch (no-op)
applyPatches > ts-block-insert: inserts rendered snippet into the named block
applyPatches > ts-block-insert: skips insertion when idempotencyKey is already present
applyPatches > ts-block-insert: throws when target file does not exist
applyPatches > ts-block-insert: defaults to no idempotency check when idempotencyKey is omitted
applyPatches > json-merge: merges dependency entries into existing package.json
applyPatches > json-merge: creates the file if it does not exist
applyPatches > json-merge: is idempotent — running twice yields the same result
applyPatches > ts-property-set: replaces a property value in the named block
applyPatches > ts-property-set: is idempotent when property already has the desired value
applyPatches > ts-property-set: throws when target file does not exist
applyPatches > skips a patch entirely when condition evaluates to falsy
applyPatches > treats a condition that traverses through a non-object value as falsy
applyPatches > does nothing for an unrecognized strategy
findAvailablePort > returns the requested port when it is free
findAvailablePort > returns the next free port when the requested one is occupied
findAvailablePort > skips multiple consecutive occupied ports
detectReact > returns true when vite.config.ts exists
detectReact > returns false when vite.config.ts does not exist
detectReact > returns false for a non-existent directory
readProjectAuthor > returns a string author field
readProjectAuthor > returns the name from an object author field
readProjectAuthor > returns undefined when author is an object without a name field
readProjectAuthor > returns undefined when author is an empty string
readProjectAuthor > returns undefined when author field is absent
readProjectAuthor > returns undefined when package.json does not exist
readProjectAuthor > returns undefined when package.json is invalid JSON
readProjectName > returns the name field from package.json
readProjectName > returns an empty string when the name field is absent
readProjectName > returns an empty string when package.json does not exist
readProjectName > returns an empty string when package.json is invalid JSON
detectPackageManager > returns "yarn" when package.json packageManager field starts with "yarn"
detectPackageManager > returns "yarn" when yarn.lock exists and there is no packageManager field
detectPackageManager > returns "npm" when there is no package.json, yarn.lock, or packageManager field
detectPackageManager > returns "npm" when package.json is invalid JSON and no yarn.lock exists
detectPackageManager > returns "npm" when packageManager field does not start with "yarn"
readGitAuthor > returns "Name <email>" when both user.name and user.email are configured
readGitAuthor > returns just the name when the email lookup fails
readGitAuthor > returns just the name when user.email resolves to an empty string
readGitAuthor > returns undefined when the user.name lookup fails (git unavailable)
readGitAuthor > returns undefined when user.name resolves to an empty string
extractDatastoreInfo > extracts name and type for each top-level datastore property
extractDatastoreInfo > handles multi-line datastore objects
extractDatastoreInfo > returns an empty array when there is no datastores block
extractDatastoreInfo > returns empty type when the datastore object has no type field
extractDatastoreInfo > does not confuse nested type fields with the top-level one
extractDatastoreInfo > ignores type values inside string literals
extractDatastoreInfo > handles escaped quotes inside string literals without ending the string early
extractDatastoreInfo > skips // line comments
readProjectDatastores > reads datastores from src/config.ts
readProjectDatastores > returns an empty array when src/config.ts does not exist
extractModelDatastore > extracts the datastore name from a @DataStore decorator
extractModelDatastore > handles double-quoted strings
extractModelDatastore > returns an empty string when no @DataStore decorator is present
readProjectModels > returns the class name (filename without .ts) for each model file
readProjectModels > excludes .d.ts declaration files
readProjectModels > returns an empty array when src/models does not exist
readModelDatastore > reads the @DataStore value from the named model file
readModelDatastore > returns an empty string when the model file does not exist
readModelDatastore > returns an empty string when the model has no @DataStore decorator
inputAuthor > always calls input() and returns whatever the user enters
inputAuthor > uses git config author as the default value for the prompt
inputAuthor > uses package.json author as the default when git config is not set and cwd is provided
inputAuthor > prefers git config over package.json when both are available
inputAuthor > uses undefined as the default when neither git config nor package.json has an author
inputAuthor > does not call readProjectAuthor when no cwd is provided
inputAuthor > does not call readProjectAuthor when git config returns an author
inputAuthor > passes the correct message and required flag to input()
generate route — route template > generates the route file and no scaffolding artifacts
generate route — route template > generates a model-extended route when model is provided
generate route — route template > generates a plain route (no model class) when model is empty
generate route — route template > uses the plain @Route decorator with no version when apiRoute is unset
generate route — route template > uses @ApiRoute with a version argument when apiRoute and apiVersion are set
generate route — route template > uses @ApiRoute with no version argument when apiRoute is set but apiVersion is empty
generate route — test template > generates the test file
generate route — test template > includes mongo-specific imports when datastoreType is mongodb
generate route — test template > omits mongo-specific imports when datastoreType is not mongodb
generate route — test template > uses the bare path as baseUrl when apiRoute is unset
generate route — test template > prefixes baseUrl with /api/v<version> when apiRoute and apiVersion are set
generate route — test template > prefixes baseUrl with /api (no version) when apiRoute is set but apiVersion is empty
generate model > generates the model file under src/models/ and no scaffolding artifacts
generate model > emits Mongo entity imports when datastoreType is mongodb
generate model > emits SQL entity imports when datastoreType is not mongodb
generate job > generates the job source and test files with no scaffolding artifacts
generate job > substitutes description, schedule, and author into the job source
generate job > references the generated job class from the test file
generate react > generates app files, route file, and vite config with no scaffolding artifacts
generate react > merges react dependencies into package.json via patch
generate react-page > generates the page component under apps/<app>/<name>/index.tsx and a flat service class, with no scaffolding artifacts
generate react-page > omits the service class when service is disabled
generate react-page > omits the client-side fetchProps helper when a service class is used
generate react-page > includes the client-side fetchProps helper when no service class is used
generate react-page > substitutes app, name, and author into the generated service class
generate react-page > nested page paths > nests the page component under the full subpath while keeping the service class flat
generate react-page > nested page paths > uses the PascalCased className for the component and service identifiers
generate server > generates core server files when all optional features are disabled
generate server > includes GitHub CI workflow when scm.github is enabled
generate server > includes database model and routes when features.hasDatabase is enabled
generate server > includes the database route tests under test/routes when features.hasDatabase is enabled
generate server > excludes the database route tests when features.hasDatabase is disabled
generate server > does not include react app files when features.react is disabled
generate server > apiRoute prefix > uses the plain @Route decorator when apiRoute is unset
generate server > apiRoute prefix > uses @ApiRoute with a version on the generated User, Auth, and Hello routes when apiRoute is set
generate default-route > only generates the route files corresponding to the enabled hasXRoute flags
generate default-route > renders the SQL ACL variant when features.mongodb is false
generate default-route > renders the Mongo ACL variant when features.mongodb is true
generate default-route > uses @ApiRoute with a version on every generated route when apiRoute is set
generate default-route > uses the plain @Route decorator when apiRoute is unset
generate default-route > generates StaticRoute.ts when hasStaticRoute is true
generate default-route > static route config.ts patch > inserts static_files as a top-level property, not nested inside another block
generate default-route > static route config.ts patch > does not patch config.ts when hasStaticRoute is false
generate default-route > static route config.ts patch > is idempotent — re-running the patch does not insert a duplicate static_files property
processTemplate > substitutes {{variable}} in file content
processTemplate > substitutes {{name}} placeholders in output file paths
processTemplate > evaluates {{#if}} Handlebars conditionals in content
processTemplate > supports the isUndefined helper for conditional blocks
processTemplate > treats a conditionalFiles condition that traverses through a non-object value as falsy
processTemplate > copies binary files (.cjs, .gz) byte-for-byte without Handlebars processing
processTemplate > excludes a file when its manifest condition is false
processTemplate > includes a file when its manifest condition is true
processTemplate > excludes a directory subtree when its manifest condition is false
processTemplate > preserves ${{ }} GitHub Actions syntax unchanged
processTemplate > throws when an output file already exists and force is not set
processTemplate > overwrites an existing output file when force is true
processTemplate > creates nested output directories for files in subdirectories
processTemplate > patches > does not copy patch template files into the output directory
processTemplate > patches > applies a json-merge patch to the project directory after file generation
processTemplate > patches > skips a patch when its condition is falsy in the context
processTemplate > patches > applies a ts-block-insert patch to src/config.ts in the project directory
processTemplate > patches > falls back to process.cwd() as the patch target when opts.projectDir is not provided
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > substitutes [[ varname ]] in Helm files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > substitutes an empty string for an undefined variable in Helm file content
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > substitutes an empty string for an undefined variable in a Helm-safe output file path
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > resolves [[#if condition]]...[[/if]] blocks in Helm files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > substitutes [[var]] placeholders in Helm-safe output file paths
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > preserves Helm {{ }} Go-template expressions in helmPaths files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > handles nested [[#if]] blocks in Helm files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > substitutes [[ dotted.path ]] from nested context in Helm files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > files outside helmPaths still use Handlebars {{ }} normally
generate default-route > context building > builds the correct context from prompts and passes it to processTemplate
generate default-route > context building > sets hasXRoute flags to true only for the routes selected via checkbox
generate default-route > context building > sets hasStaticRoute: true when static-route is selected via checkbox
generate default-route > api flag > sets apiRoute: false and apiVersion: undefined when the "Is this an API route?" prompt is declined
generate default-route > api flag > sets apiRoute: true and apiVersion from the follow-up prompt when confirmed
generate default-route > api flag > --api with a value skips both the confirm and version prompts
generate default-route > --type flag > generates only the specified route type without showing the checkbox prompt
generate default-route > --type flag > accepts each of the documented route types
generate default-route > --type flag > rejects an unrecognized --type value
generate default-route > --type flag > generates multiple route types when --type is passed more than once
generate default-route > --type flag > dedupes repeated --type values
generate default-route > --type flag > rejects when any of multiple --type values is invalid, listing all invalid ones
generate default-route > static route type > prompts for the static file path when static-route is selected via checkbox and --static-path is not provided
generate default-route > static route type > does not prompt for the static file path when static-route is not selected
generate default-route > static route type > --static-path skips the interactive prompt and uses the flag value
generate default-route > static route type > --type static without --static-path still prompts for the path interactively
generate default-route > static route type > --type static --static-path is fully non-interactive
generate default-route > static route type > defaults staticPath to "public" in the context even when static-route is not part of the selection
generate default-route > mongodb detection for the ACL route > sets features.mongodb to false when the project has no mongodb datastore
generate default-route > mongodb detection for the ACL route > sets features.mongodb to true when the project has a mongodb datastore
generate default-route > mongodb detection for the ACL route > reads datastores from the current working directory
generate default-route > author resolution > uses the --author flag and skips inputAuthor entirely
generate default-route > author resolution > calls inputAuthor with the project cwd and uses its return value
generate default-route > output and template options > uses process.cwd() as the default output directory
generate default-route > output and template options > uses --output-dir when provided
generate default-route > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate default-route > output and template options > points processTemplate at the default-route template directory
generate default-route > error handling > propagates an error thrown by processTemplate
generate default-route > error handling > falls back to String(err) when processTemplate rejects with a non-Error value
generate docker > context building > passes datastores from the project config into context
generate docker > context building > includes the current year in context
generate docker > context building > includes project_name from package.json in context
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets hasMongoDB true when any datastore type is mongodb
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets hasPostgres true when any datastore type is postgresql
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets hasRedis true when any datastore type is redis
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets all boolean flags true when all three types are present
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets all boolean flags false when datastores is empty
generate docker > database boolean flags > ignores unknown datastore types for boolean flags
generate docker > output and template options > uses process.cwd() as the output directory when --output-dir is not set
generate docker > output and template options > uses --output-dir as the output directory and project source when provided
generate docker > output and template options > points processTemplate at the docker template directory
generate docker > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate docker > output and template options > passes projectDir: process.cwd() to processTemplate
generate docker > output and template options > calls readProjectDatastores with the current working directory
generate docker > output and template options > calls readProjectName with the current working directory
generate docker > package manager context > sets pkgMgr.npm true and pkgMgr.yarn false when detectPackageManager returns npm
generate docker > package manager context > sets pkgMgr.yarn true and pkgMgr.npm false when detectPackageManager returns yarn
generate docker > package manager context > calls detectPackageManager with the project cwd
generate docker > error handling > propagates an error thrown by processTemplate
generate docker > error handling > falls back to String(err) when processTemplate rejects with a non-Error value
generate job > context building > builds the correct context from prompts and passes it to processTemplate
generate job > context building > includes project_name from the project config in the context
generate job > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --description skips the description input prompt
generate job > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --schedule skips the schedule input prompt
generate job > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --author skips inputAuthor entirely
generate job > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > skips all prompts when description, schedule, and author flags are all provided
generate job > author resolution > calls inputAuthor with the project cwd and uses its return value
generate job > output and template options > uses process.cwd() as the default output directory
generate job > output and template options > uses --output-dir when provided
generate job > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate job > output and template options > passes projectDir: cwd to processTemplate
generate job > output and template options > points processTemplate at the job template directory
generate job > error handling > propagates an error thrown by processTemplate
generate job > error handling > falls back to String(err) when processTemplate rejects with a non-Error value
generate k8s > context building > passes datastores from the project config into context
generate k8s > context building > includes the current year in context
generate k8s > context building > includes project_name from package.json in context
generate k8s > database boolean flags > sets hasMongoDB true when any datastore type is mongodb
generate k8s > database boolean flags > sets hasPostgres true when any datastore type is postgresql
generate k8s > database boolean flags > sets hasRedis true when any datastore type is redis
generate k8s > database boolean flags > sets all boolean flags true when all three types are present
generate k8s > database boolean flags > sets all boolean flags false when datastores is empty
generate k8s > database boolean flags > ignores unknown datastore types for boolean flags
generate k8s > output and template options > uses process.cwd() as the output directory when --output-dir is not set
generate k8s > output and template options > uses --output-dir as the output directory and project source when provided
generate k8s > output and template options > points processTemplate at the helm template directory
generate k8s > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate k8s > output and template options > passes projectDir: process.cwd() to processTemplate
generate k8s > output and template options > calls readProjectDatastores with the current working directory
generate k8s > output and template options > calls readProjectName with the current working directory
generate k8s > error handling > propagates an error thrown by processTemplate
generate k8s > error handling > falls back to String(err) when processTemplate rejects with a non-Error value
generate model > context building > builds the correct context from prompts and passes it to processTemplate
generate model > context building > prompts for a cache TTL when --cache is omitted entirely and the user enables caching
generate model > context building > includes protect: false when the protect prompt answers no
generate model > context building > disables caching when the user declines the enable-caching prompt
generate model > context building > sets isMongoDb true and other db booleans false when datastoreType is mongodb
generate model > context building > includes project_name from package.json in the context
generate model > datastore selection — configured datastores present > shows only non-acl datastores in the select choices, plus a new option
generate model > datastore selection — configured datastores present > resolves datastoreType from the config list for the selected name
generate model > datastore selection — configured datastores present > resolves an empty datastoreType when the selected name is not in the configured list
generate model > datastore selection — configured datastores present > selecting "+ New datastore..." prompts for db type and uses it as both name and type
generate model > datastore selection — configured datastores present > does not show the "set up new database" prompt when datastores are configured
generate model > datastore selection — no configured datastores > asks to set up a new database and selects the type when the user says yes
generate model > datastore selection — no configured datastores > sets datastore and datastoreType to empty string when the user declines
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --datastore skips the datastore select but still resolves datastoreType from config
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --datastore leaves datastoreType empty when the name is not in the project config
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --description skips the description input prompt
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --cache with a value overrides the default TTL and skips the cache prompt
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --cache with no value defaults to "60" and skips the cache prompt
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --cache with no value still resolves to "60" when immediately followed by another flag
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > explicitly setting --cache to an empty string still triggers the enable-caching prompt
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --protect skips the protect confirm prompt
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --author skips inputAuthor entirely
generate model > author resolution > calls inputAuthor with the project cwd and uses its return value
generate model > output and template options > uses process.cwd() as the default output directory
generate model > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate model > output and template options > points processTemplate at the model template directory
generate model > docker and helm subcommands after new datastore > does not call GenerateDocker or GenerateHelm when an existing datastore is selected
generate model > docker and helm subcommands after new datastore > offers to update docker when a new datastore is added and docker-compose.yml exists
generate model > docker and helm subcommands after new datastore > skips docker update when the user declines
generate model > docker and helm subcommands after new datastore > offers to update helm when a new datastore is added and helm/Chart.yaml exists
generate model > docker and helm subcommands after new datastore > skips helm update when the user declines
generate model > docker and helm subcommands after new datastore > offers to update both docker and helm when both exist for a new datastore
generate model > error handling > propagates an error thrown by processTemplate
generate model > error handling > falls back to String(err) when processTemplate rejects with a non-Error value
generate react-page > context building > builds the correct context from prompts and passes it to processTemplate
generate react-page > context building > includes service: false when the service prompt answers no
generate react-page > context building > includes project_name from the project config in the context
generate react-page > className derivation from a (possibly nested) page path > keeps name as the raw path but PascalCases className for a nested path
generate react-page > className derivation from a (possibly nested) page path > capitalizes a single lowercase segment
generate react-page > className derivation from a (possibly nested) page path > joins hyphen- and underscore-separated words within a segment
generate react-page > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --service skips the service confirm prompt
generate react-page > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --author skips inputAuthor entirely
generate react-page > author resolution > calls inputAuthor with the project cwd and uses its return value
generate react-page > output and template options > uses process.cwd() as the default output directory
generate react-page > output and template options > uses --output-dir when provided
generate react-page > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate react-page > output and template options > passes projectDir: process.cwd() to processTemplate
generate react-page > output and template options > points processTemplate at the react-page template directory
generate react-page > error handling > propagates an error thrown by processTemplate
generate react-page > error handling > falls back to String(err) when processTemplate rejects with a non-Error value
generate react > context building > builds the correct context from prompts and passes it to processTemplate
generate react > context building > includes hydrate: false when the hydrate select answers no
generate react > context building > includes project_name from package.json in the context
generate react > context building > uses the app name as the default suggestion for the path prompt
generate react > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --path skips the path input prompt
generate react > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --hydrate skips the hydrate confirm prompt
generate react > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --author skips inputAuthor entirely
generate react > author resolution > calls inputAuthor with the project cwd and uses its return value
generate react > output and template options > uses the project root as the default output directory
generate react > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate react > output and template options > passes projectDir: process.cwd() to processTemplate
generate react > output and template options > points processTemplate at the react template directory
generate react > error handling > propagates an error thrown by processTemplate
generate react > error handling > falls back to String(err) when processTemplate rejects with a non-Error value
generate route > context building > builds the correct context from prompts and passes it to processTemplate
generate route > context building > resolves datastoreType from the model's @DataStore decorator via the project config
generate route > context building > leaves datastoreType empty when the model's datastore is not in the project config
generate route > context building > sets hasRedis true when a redis datastore is configured alongside the model datastore
generate route > context building > sets datastore and datastoreType to empty when model is "(none)"
generate route > context building > leaves datastoreType empty when the model has no @DataStore decorator
generate route > api flag > sets apiRoute: false and apiVersion: undefined when the "Is this an API route?" prompt is declined
generate route > api flag > sets apiRoute: true and apiVersion from the follow-up prompt when confirmed
generate route > api flag > --api with a value skips both the confirm and version prompts
generate route > api flag > --api with an empty string still triggers the "Is this an API route?" prompt, but apiRoute stays true since the flag was passed
generate route > model selection > shows a select with "(none)", project models, and a "+ New model..." option
generate route > model selection > runs generate model inline when "+ New model..." is selected and uses the newly created model
generate route > model selection > falls back to an empty model name when no new model is detected after generate model runs
generate route > model selection > falls back to a free-form input when no models exist in the project
generate route > model selection > --no-model skips all model prompts and leaves model undefined in context
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --model skips the model select and resolves datastore from the named model file
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --description skips the description input prompt
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --path skips the route path input prompt
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --protect skips the protect confirm prompt
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --author skips inputAuthor entirely
generate route > author resolution > calls inputAuthor with the project cwd and uses its return value
generate route > output and template options > uses project root as the default output directory for the route file
generate route > output and template options > passes force: true to both processTemplate calls when --force is set
generate route > error handling > propagates an error thrown by processTemplate
generate route > error handling > falls back to String(err) when processTemplate rejects with a non-Error value
generate server > builds the correct base context from prompts
generate server > maps selected database and feature choices to boolean flags on context.features
generate server > sets hasDatabase true when any persistent store is selected
generate server > sets hasDatabase false when only non-persistent features are selected
generate server > maps the SCM choice to a boolean map on context.scm
generate server > sets scm.git and scm.gitlab true when scm is "gitlab"
generate server > maps the package manager choice to a boolean map on context.pkgMgr
generate server > uses ./<name> as the default output directory
generate server > uses --output-dir when provided
generate server > points processTemplate at the server template directory
generate server > passes force: true when --force is set
generate server > api flag > sets apiRoute: false and apiVersion: undefined when the api prefix prompt is declined
generate server > api flag > sets apiRoute: true and apiVersion from the follow-up prompt when confirmed
generate server > default routes subcommand > does not call GenerateDefaultRoute when no route-* features are selected
generate server > default routes subcommand > calls GenerateDefaultRoute once with a --type per selected route-* feature
generate server > default routes subcommand > passes --force to GenerateDefaultRoute when --force is set on the server command
generate server > default routes subcommand > passes --api to GenerateDefaultRoute when the api prefix prompt was confirmed
generate server > default routes subcommand > does not pass --api to GenerateDefaultRoute when the api prefix prompt was declined
generate server > default routes subcommand > uses the default output directory (./<name>) when --output-dir is not set
generate server > author resolution > calls inputAuthor and uses its return value as the author
generate server > author resolution > --author skips inputAuthor and uses the flag value
generate server > author resolution > passes the resolved author through to GenerateDefaultRoute for selected route-* features
generate server > docker subcommand > runs GenerateDocker with --output-dir after server generation when docker is selected
generate server > docker subcommand > does not run GenerateDocker when docker is not selected
generate server > docker subcommand > passes --force to GenerateDocker when --force is set on the server command
generate server > docker subcommand > uses the default output directory (./<name>) when --output-dir is not set
generate server > helm subcommand > runs GenerateHelm with --output-dir after server generation when k8s is selected
generate server > helm subcommand > does not run GenerateHelm when k8s is not selected
generate server > helm subcommand > passes --force to GenerateHelm when --force is set on the server command
generate server > helm subcommand > uses the default output directory (./<name>) when --output-dir is not set
generate server > helm subcommand > runs both GenerateDocker and GenerateHelm when both features are selected
generate server > react subcommand > runs GenerateReact with --output-dir after server generation when react is selected
generate server > react subcommand > does not run GenerateReact when react is not selected
generate server > react subcommand > passes --force to GenerateReact when --force is set on the server command
generate server > react subcommand > uses the default output directory (./<name>) when --output-dir is not set
generate server > react subcommand > runs docker, helm, react, and default-route subcommands when all are selected
generate server > error handling > propagates an error thrown by processTemplate
generate server > error handling > falls back to String(err) when processTemplate rejects with a non-Error value
